facts: The Andromeda galaxy is approaching us at 250,000 miles per hour
It's equivalent to 220,000 football fields lined up side by side in size
The distance between the two galaxies is about 2.5 million times the distance from New York to Los Angeles
idea.payoff: and we still don't know what will happen when they merge
idea.title: Galaxies on Collision Course 🌌
idea.description: The Milky Way and Andromeda galaxies are on a catastrophic collision course 🌌! As they hurtle through space at mind-boggling speeds, the question remains: what will happen when they finally meet? 🤔 Will we witness a cosmic explosion or a majestic dance of stars? 🌟 Watch to find out more about this intergalactic impending doom 🔭

scenes.visual: Two distinct spiral galaxies (representing Milky Way and Andromeda) slowly drift closer towards the center of the screen, with a dark, star-filled space background.
Focus on the Andromeda galaxy (larger, more distinct spiral) with a subtle motion blur or 'speed lines' indicating rapid movement. A small, faint Milky Way galaxy is visible in the distance.

An abstract, stylized representation of a galaxy disk. On the right, a long line of connected football field icons stretches horizontally, disappearing off-screen to imply vastness. A 'x 220,000' text floats above the fields.

A graphic depicting the Earth with a small line connecting New York and Los Angeles. This graphic then shrinks and repeats across the screen, forming a dotted line that stretches immensely, connecting two tiny galaxy icons at its ends.

scenes.text: What happens? We still don't know.
scenes.visual: The two galaxies are shown in an intermediate stage of collision, with gas and stars visibly distorting and merging. The background nebula darkens slightly, and a large, stylized question mark icon slowly fades in over the chaotic merging galaxies.
